step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("evidence that dedicated discharge coordinators improve hospital discharge quality")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

(Only two sentences maximum. Then state your answer choice.)
A dedicated discharge coordinator improves hospital discharge quality by systematically coordinating follow-up care, patient education, and medication management, resulting in measurable reductions in 30-day and 90-day readmission rates—as demonstrated by the Care Transition Intervention (CTI) program. Multiple studies confirm that structured care coordination, including visits post-discharge and phone check-ins, significantly lowers readmission risk and enhances continuity of care, especially for vulnerable populations like the elderly and post-cardiac patients. \boxed{{A}}
